// NOTE: Recommended ESLint configuration for React + TypeScript projects
// USAGE: Copy relevant sections to your .eslintrc.json or adapt to .eslintrc.js format

rules.// EXPLANATION: Enforces Rules of Hooks - prevents conditional hook calls, loops, nested functions
rules.// PREVENTS: Top 10 Mistake #3 - Violating Rules of Hooks
rules.// EXPLANATION: Ensures all dependencies are included in useEffect/useMemo/useCallback arrays
rules.// PREVENTS: Top 10 Mistake #1 - Missing/incorrect dependencies (infinite loops, stale closures)
rules.// EXPLANATION: Requires key prop when rendering lists with .map()
rules.// PREVENTS: Top 10 Mistake #2 - Missing keys (leads to using index as key)
rules.// EXPLANATION: Warns against using array index as key prop
rules.// PREVENTS: Top 10 Mistake #2 - Using array index as key (state corruption)
rules.// EXPLANATION: Prevents direct state mutation (for class components)
Warns about creating functions in render (performance issue)
rules.// PREVENTS: Unnecessary re-renders from new function references
Top 10 Mistake #6 - Incorrect TypeScript event types (leads to using 'any')
rules.// EXPLANATION: Don't require return types on every function (inference works well)
rules.// RATIONALE: TypeScript can infer most return types, explicit types on props interfaces is sufficient
rules.// EXPLANATION: Disable PropTypes checking when using TypeScript
rules.// RATIONALE: TypeScript provides compile-time type checking, PropTypes are runtime
rules.// EXPLANATION: Don't require 'import React' in every file (React 17+ JSX transform)
rules.// RATIONALE: React 17+ has automatic JSX runtime, import not needed
rules.// EXPLANATION: Ensures <a> tags have valid href or use <button> instead
Warns about console.log (but allows console.warn and console.error)
rules.// PREVENTS: Leaving debug logs in production code
rules.// EXPLANATION: Error on unused variables (but allow _ prefix for intentionally unused)
Prefer const over let when variable is never reassigned
Disallow var, require let or const
rules.// RATIONALE: var has confusing scoping rules, deprecated in modern JavaScript

// OPTIONAL STRICTER RULES: Uncomment these for more strict linting

// rules-strict.// EXPLANATION: Enforces function declarations for named components (consistency)
Require self-closing tags when no children (<Component /> not <Component></Component>)
Disallows explicit 'true' value for boolean props (use <Component required /> not <Component required={true} />)

// INTEGRATION.pre-commit: Use husky + lint-staged to run ESLint before commits
// INTEGRATION.ci-cd: Add 'npm run lint' to CI pipeline (fails build on errors)

// TROUBLESHOOTING.react-version-warning: Add settings.react.version: 'detect' to auto-detect React version
// TROUBLESHOOTING.hooks-rules-not-working: Ensure eslint-plugin-react-hooks is installed and plugin is listed
